Content: Brown: Good, a little more slowly.
Content_zh: Brown：很好，再慢一點。

Content: Technician: And, 115.
Content_zh: Technician：然後，115。

Content: Kaufman: Not quite.
Content_zh: Kaufman：還不太對。

Content: We won't activate the Omicron Inhibitor until we're ready to take the shields down.
Content_zh: 等我們準備好解除護盾，才會啟動 Omicron Inhibitor。

Content: Brown: Mm!
Content_zh: Brown：嗯！

Content: Yes.
Content_zh: 對。

Content: Sorry.
Content_zh: 抱歉。

Content: I'm steppin' on you, but: Wow. Yum.
Content_zh: 我插到你的話了，不過：哇，好讚。

Content: Schafer: "Wow. Yum." Brown: Dude, I love when--
Content_zh: Schafer：「哇，好讚。」Brown：老兄，我超愛那種……

Content: When they get it right, it's like, fuck.
Content_zh: 他們一抓到感覺，就會讓人覺得，靠。

Content: Schafer: I want to eat your voice when you poop it out.
Content_zh: Schafer：你把聲音拉出來的時候，我想把它吃掉。

Content: Brown: It's the best.
Content_zh: Brown：那最棒了。

Content: Just fucking poopin' in my mouth.
Content_zh: 就他媽直接拉到我嘴裡。

Content: I'm saying that just for you.
Content_zh: 我是特地說給你聽的。

Content: [Music] McConnel: Um...
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：嗯……

Content: I just really like to... to be here whenever I can make it up the...hill.
Content_zh: 只要我爬得上這座……山，我就很喜歡來這裡。

Content: It is a little reminiscent of the field that the girl starts out in, now, isn't it?
Content_zh: 這裡有點像那個女孩一開始所在的田野，對吧？

Content: When I did that theme, um...we hadn't moved yet.
Content_zh: 我寫那段主題時，嗯……我們還沒搬過來。

Content: Uh, so I sorta had to imagine that field and work to the picture, um...
Content_zh: 所以我算是得一邊想像那片田野，一邊照著畫面創作。

Content: But you know, uh...
Content_zh: 不過你知道……

Content: The idea of a...
Content_zh: 那種……

Content: of a beautiful field with solitude that you can go to to just get away from everything
Content_zh: 有一片漂亮又安靜的田野，可以讓你暫時遠離一切。

Content: and with a little village below is-is definitely, uh...
Content_zh: 下面還有個小村莊，這確實……

Content: you know, definitely resonates.
Content_zh: 確實很有共鳴。

Content: So maybe I...now that we're moving back to the girl's world, maybe it's time for me to take more of these walks.
Content_zh: 所以也許……既然現在要回到女孩的世界，也許我該多出來這樣走走。

Content: [Music] Schafer: What is happening here?
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：這裡是怎麼回事？

Content: What has been going on?
Content_zh: 最近都發生了什麼？

Content: I've been so isolated writing in here.
Content_zh: 我一直關在這裡寫東西，完全與世隔絕。

Content: What else has been going on?
Content_zh: 還發生了什麼？

Content: Every time I play the game, it's got new crazy stuff in it, which looks awesome.
Content_zh: 我每次玩，都會看到新的瘋狂東西，而且看起來超棒。

Content: Franzke: So here's the start screen.
Content_zh: Franzke：這是開始畫面。

Content: And if I can basically linear blend into 8-bit mode and come out again and go back.
Content_zh: 我基本上可以線性混合進 8-bit 模式，再切回來。

Content: And the game is actually full--fully playable.
Content_zh: 而且遊戲其實已經完整可玩了。

Content: Schafer: I don't know. Oliver just snuck that in over the weekend.
Content_zh: Schafer：我也不知道。Oliver 週末偷偷把那個加進去了。

Content: He's like, "Hey, hit this-- hit this key." And I was like, "Why?" And I hit it.
Content_zh: 他說：「嘿，按這個，按這個鍵。」我說：「為什麼？」然後就按了。

Content: It was like, pow, awesome.
Content_zh: 結果砰一下，超棒。

Content: Franzke: Um...and there are a bunch of things that are happening.
Content_zh: Franzke：嗯……這裡其實做了很多事。

Content: I mean, obviously it's pixelated, but we're also, like, kind of compressing
Content_zh: 顯然它會像素化，但我們也有點像是在壓縮

Content: the color range as well, like in the old days.
Content_zh: 色彩範圍，像以前那樣。

Content: So we're kind of trying to emulate that you only have 256 colors.
Content_zh: 所以我們是在模擬只有 256 色的感覺。

Content: Like, since the beginning of the project, I was like, well, you know, I really like the old-school games.
Content_zh: 從專案一開始，我就想，我真的很喜歡老派遊戲。

Content: I like the pixelated art style that we had back then.
Content_zh: 我喜歡以前那種像素美術風格。

Content: Um...and then I was like, well, I wonder if I can just, like, take the image and do some
Content_zh: 然後我就在想，能不能直接拿畫面來做一些

Content: just shader work to it to, like, make it look like, you know, it's a game made in the '90s sometime,
Content_zh: shader 處理，讓它看起來像 90 年代某個時候做的遊戲，

Content: like a little bit 8-bit.
Content_zh: 有點 8-bit 的感覺。

Content: Uh, and it works pretty well, actually.
Content_zh: 而且實際效果還滿不錯的。

Content: So far it seems okay, actually.
Content_zh: 目前看來還不錯

Content: I thought I would be way more stressed out.
Content_zh: 我以為自己會緊張得多。

Content: So far it's okay, and probably, uh, when you do the-- when you can do the whole cutting thing,
Content_zh: 目前還好。也許等你們剪片的時候，

Content: you can cut, like, you know, months in the future; I will be sitting here like, oh my God!
Content_zh: 可以剪到幾個月後，我就會坐在這裡說：「我的天啊！」

Content: Schafer: And the team is bringing up the last parts of "Act 1" to alpha level.
Content_zh: Schafer：團隊正在把《第一幕》最後幾個部分推到 Alpha 水準。

Content: Um, starting work on the pyramid.
Content_zh: 嗯，開始處理金字塔。

Content: There's a pyramid in the game that's getting brought to alpha this week,
Content_zh: 遊戲裡有座金字塔，這週要做到 Alpha。

Content: and it's looking awesome.
Content_zh: 看起來很棒

Content: Well, alpha is just that there's something rough in there representing everything.
Content_zh: Alpha 就是每個東西都有粗略的版本先放進去。

Content: There's like, mm-- all the assets are, like-- Something's in there taking a place for them.
Content_zh: 所有資產都先有東西佔位。

Content: And then--and then you get them all workin' the way you want them to for beta.
Content_zh: 然後到 Beta，你再讓它們照你想要的方式運作。

Content: And then after beta, you're hopefully just fixing bugs.
Content_zh: Beta 之後，希望就只是在修 bug。

Content: Way ahead of schedule.
Content_zh: 進度超前很多。

Content: Rice: We're--we're good.
Content_zh: Rice：我們……我們沒問題。

Content: We're where I said we should be.
Content_zh: 我們就在我說該到的位置。

Content: Schafer: Well, things move quick once you-- You know, you have certain deadlines.
Content_zh: Schafer：有些期限一出來，事情就會推得很快。

Content: Like, we wanted to get a bunch of, um, voice done so we could get a PAX trailer.
Content_zh: 像是我們想先錄好一批配音，好做 PAX 預告片。

Content: And, um, also 'cause the animators are waiting for the voice to start--
Content_zh: 還有，動畫師也在等配音才能開始……

Content: They haven't finished, like, a single scene in the game yet 'cause then you want the final voice in there.
Content_zh: 他們還沒真正完成任何一場戲，因為要等正式配音放進去。

Content: So we wanna get that done, and once it starts rolling, it, um, it goes pretty quickly.
Content_zh: 所以我們想把那件事完成，而一旦開始動起來，就會進展很快。

Content: Crook: You know, when they play, go through the playthroughs and stuff right now, sort of the animation
Content_zh: Crook：你知道，他們現在跑完整遊玩流程時，動畫常常

Content: is sort of the least polished, oftentimes.
Content_zh: 是最不完整的部分。

Content: And, um, and so sometimes it's rough, like, to sit through playthroughs, you know,
Content_zh: 所以有時候坐著看完整流程會有點難受，

Content: and-and know that the quality is not there yet just 'cause you haven't gotten to that phase.
Content_zh: 因為你知道品質還沒到位，只是因為還沒進到那個階段。

Content: Schafer: Greg came in; he has a question for us.
Content_zh: Schafer：Greg 進來了，他有問題要問我們。

Content: Brown: Greg! Rice: Hi.
Content_zh: Brown：Greg！Rice：嗨。

Content: Brown: How are you doing?
Content_zh: Brown：你好嗎？

Content: Rice: I am doing well, how are you?
Content_zh: Rice：我很好，你好嗎？

Content: Brown: Hey, I'm good.
Content_zh: Brown：我很好

Content: Rice: Sweet. Um, no, I just, uh, I got an email from our new executive assistant
Content_zh: Rice：太好了。嗯，我剛收到新行政助理寄來的信，

Content: that was trying to set up Tim's flights, so I wanted to see how things were looking
Content_zh: 她想安排 Tim 的班機，所以我想先看看狀況

Content: and if she should go ahead and do that.
Content_zh: 確認她是不是可以繼續訂。

Content: Brown: So if each one of these characters has at least 80 lines to read, then, yes.
Content_zh: Brown：如果每個角色至少都有 80 句要錄，那就可以。

Content: Rice: I think we're gonna have enough written.
Content_zh: Rice：我想我們寫好的量會夠。

Content: Schafer: So that's a question for Malena or someone with access to the database.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以這要問 Malena，或是有資料庫權限的人。

Content: Rice: We can have Malena run that right now.
Content_zh: Rice：我們可以現在請 Malena 跑一下資料。

Content: Schafer: Okay. Today we're gonna start.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。今天我們要開始了。

Content: Please welcome Malena.
Content_zh: 請歡迎 Malena。

Content: Malena Annable.
Content_zh: Malena Annable。

Content: Annable: I'm so happy to be here with you guys.
Content_zh: Annable：很高興能和大家在這裡。

Content: Schafer: Here in person. She's come through the magic of the screen to our world.
Content_zh: Schafer：而且是本人到場。她穿越螢幕的魔法，來到我們的世界。

Content: Just like the monsters in "Pacific Rim." She is a producer,
Content_zh: 就像《Pacific Rim》裡的怪獸一樣。她是製作人，

Content: and she specializes in localization and things involving the dialogue database.
Content_zh: 專門負責在地化，以及對話資料庫相關的事情。

Content: Um...even though she's up in Portland, she remotely tracks all that stuff
Content_zh: 雖然她人在 Portland，但她會遠端追蹤這些東西，

Content: and she does localization for all of our games.
Content_zh: 也負責我們所有遊戲的在地化。

Content: And, um, she is the one pushing on me for the writing deadline to get it into Khris' hands, 'cause it kinda goes,
Content_zh: 她就是一直催我趕寫作期限，好把內容交到 Khris 手上的人。因為流程大概是，

Content: she gets the raw writing and puts it in the database, and, um, and gets the script all ready to hand off to Khris.
Content_zh: 她拿到原始稿，把它放進資料庫，再把劇本整理好交給 Khris。

Content: That's a good question; why is she here?
Content_zh: 這問題問得好，她為什麼會在這裡？

Content: Maybe she's up to something.
Content_zh: 也許她有什麼盤算。

Content: Annable: Yeah, I wanna talk to you about more dialogue things and...
Content_zh: Annable：對，我想跟你談更多對話的事，還有……

Content: Schafer: I have to write more dialogue? Annable: Yeah, you do.
Content_zh: Schafer：我還得寫更多對話？Annable：對，沒錯。

Content: Schafer: God. Annable: You don't want me to write it.
Content_zh: Schafer：天啊。Annable：你不會想讓我寫的。

Content: Schafer: Gotta be done by now. It's gotta be done by now.
Content_zh: Schafer：現在應該要寫完了。現在應該要寫完了。

Content: Annable: So, yeah, so I'm-I'm busy with that all week.
Content_zh: Annable：所以對，我這整週都在忙這個。

Content: I'm happy to see everyone. Schafer: But if I write it this week, it'll screw up the schedule, 'cause...
Content_zh: 很高興見到大家。Schafer：可是如果我這週才寫，會搞亂時程，因為……

Content: Okay.
Content_zh: 好吧

Content: Annable: So I want Tim to write in the way that whatever feels most comfortable to him.
Content_zh: Annable：所以我希望 Tim 用他覺得最舒服的方式寫。

Content: And as fast as possible.
Content_zh: 而且越快越好。

Content: Dialogue is so important to our games, so making sure that we have, like, a really strong system--
Content_zh: 對話對我們的遊戲非常重要，所以要確保我們有一套很強的系統。

Content: I have--there's a dialogue database that Paul Du Bois built and it's amazing.
Content_zh: Paul Du Bois 做了一個對話資料庫，非常厲害。

Content: And it's just--for me, it's just management of, like, you know, tens of thousands of files.
Content_zh: 對我來說，重點就是管理好幾萬個檔案。

Content: I mean, "Brütal Legend," there are over 35,000 lines of dialogue,
Content_zh: 像《Brütal Legend》就有三萬五千多句對話，

Content: and I had to manage that across five languages.
Content_zh: 而且我要管理五種語言的版本。

Content: And...it wasn't that bad.
Content_zh: 而且…… 也不算太糟

Content: Rice: But, yeah, we could have Malena check out current status of lines per character.
Content_zh: Rice：不過對，我們可以請 Malena 查一下目前每個角色有多少句台詞。

Content: And so if that happens, you think, um, like, casting-wise we'll be ready?
Content_zh: 如果是這樣，你覺得選角方面會準備好了嗎？

Content: Brown: Um, I think we will. I think we're, like-- I think by the end of this meeting
Content_zh: Brown：嗯，我想會。我想這場會議結束前，

Content: we will know who we're gonna choose for everything, and then it'll be me calling and checking avails
Content_zh: 我們就會知道每個角色要選誰，接著我會打電話確認

Content: on them all for next week.
Content_zh: 他們下週有沒有空。

Content: Schafer: And then a lot of our friends are coming back.
Content_zh: Schafer：而且很多老朋友會回來。

Content: Richard Horvitz, the voice of Raz, is gonna be doing some voices.
Content_zh: Raz 的配音 Richard Horvitz 會來配幾個聲音。

Content: And Jennifer Hale, um, from "Brütal Legend" and "Mass Effect," is gonna be--
Content_zh: 還有《Brütal Legend》和《Mass Effect》的 Jennifer Hale 會……

Content: Yes, Shepard is gonna be doing, uh, I think the Mom computer.
Content_zh: 對，Shepard 應該會配媽媽電腦。

Content: Um...and that's all we have to announce right now as far as voice, but that's very exciting stuff.
Content_zh: 配音方面目前能公布的就這些，但真的很令人興奮。

Content: Get that all wrapped up so we'll actually have something that we can put into the game and show at PAX.
Content_zh: 把這些都搞定後，我們才真的有東西能放進遊戲，拿去 PAX 展示。

Content: This weekend?
Content_zh: 這個週末？

Content: This weekend, oh my God, it's coming up so soon.
Content_zh: 這週末，我的天，快到了

Content: We're flying down to LA to record the voice for the game, at least a third of it.
Content_zh: 我們要飛去洛杉磯錄遊戲配音，至少錄三分之一。

Content: It's a third of the voice for the game.
Content_zh: 是遊戲配音的三分之一。

Content: And we've got some, um, interesting people coming down to do voice.
Content_zh: 而且有一些很有意思的人要來配音。

Content: Some old friends, some hopefully new friends.
Content_zh: 有些是老朋友，有些希望會成為新朋友。

Content: We'll be locked in a room together, me and Khris Brown, for four days.
Content_zh: 我和 Khris Brown 會一起被關在房間裡四天。

Content: [Music] Horvitz: There is no room in the Space Weaver's life for friendship.
Content_zh: [音樂] Horvitz：Space Weaver 的生命中沒有友情的位置。

Content: Brown: Aww... Horvitz: He slides alone, like a shuttle of yarn across the warp of time
Content_zh: Brown：喔……Horvitz：他孤獨滑行，像毛線梭穿越時間的經緯，

Content: on an ever so--shortening-- Schafer: Fucked that up.
Content_zh: 在一條越來越……短的……Schafer：搞砸了。

Content: How can you fuck that up?
Content_zh: 你怎麼會把那個搞砸？

Content: You totally fucked that up.
Content_zh: 你完全搞砸了。

Content: Horvitz: I love that!
Content_zh: Horvitz：我喜歡！

Content: That is so fucking good.
Content_zh: 真他媽好極了

Content: All right. Here we go.
Content_zh: 好吧 好了

Content: Schafer: It's kinda like he's Pee Wee, rebel, loner.
Content_zh: Schafer：他有點像 Pee Wee，叛逆、孤僻。

Content: Technician: And, 24.
Content_zh: Technician：然後，24。

Content: Horvitz: Look, see, see, look at this, look at this island in front right here, see?
Content_zh: Horvitz：看，看，看，看，看這個，看前面的這個島，看到嗎？

Content: And it's just like this hairline's receding.
Content_zh: 就像這條發線在退縮

Content: I can't stand it.
Content_zh: 我受不了

Content: Brown: Really? Schafer: Lice check? Lice check?
Content_zh: Brown：真的？ 蝨子檢查？ 蝨子檢查？

Content: Brown: It looks good. Horvitz: No, not...
Content_zh: Brown：看起來不錯 不，不是……

Content: Brown: Tim, you have lice.
Content_zh: Brown：蒂姆 你有蝨子

Content: We have never cast a game this quickly.
Content_zh: 我們從來沒有這麼快完成一款遊戲的選角。

Content: Um, and we have never cast a game with, um, a budget this limited.
Content_zh: 也從來沒有在這麼有限的預算下替遊戲選角。

Content: I mean, it-- with the exception of, you know, with "Day of the Tentacle," you have, like,
Content_zh: 除了《Day of the Tentacle》那種，大概只有

Content: 1,500 lines, you know what I mean?
Content_zh: 1500 句台詞，你懂嗎？

Content: But even then, I think, um...
Content_zh: 但即使那樣，我想……

Content: I think we probably spent more money on "Day of the Tentacle." That's Tim, who started the company,
Content_zh: 我們在《Day of the Tentacle》上花的錢可能還更多。Tim 創辦了這家公司，

Content: and he was one of the chief game designers at LucasArts, working on, like, "Monkey Island"
Content_zh: 也是 LucasArts 的主要遊戲設計師之一，做過《Monkey Island》

Content: and kind of this whole, um, set or series of games
Content_zh: 以及那一整批、一整系列

Content: that people feel really strongly about.
Content_zh: 讓大家非常有感情的遊戲。

Content: They have a really strong emotional connection to those games, and then we've continued...
Content_zh: 人們和那些遊戲有很深的情感連結，而我們後來也延續了……

Content: I started at LucasArts in 1990.
Content_zh: 我 1990 年進 LucasArts。

Content: And there were 60 of us.
Content_zh: 當時我們只有 60 個人。

Content: Like, LucasArts was 60 people.
Content_zh: LucasArts 總共才 60 人。

Content: I mean, we were a bunch of goofball rejects.
Content_zh: 我們就是一群怪咖邊緣人。

Content: It's not--it's not how it is now, where you have guys who go to college
Content_zh: 不像現在，有人上大學，

Content: and then video games is a place where you can be successful financially.
Content_zh: 然後把電玩當成可以賺大錢的產業。

Content: Do you know what I mean?
Content_zh: 你知道我的意思嗎？

Content: We were all kids who were alone...
Content_zh: 我們都是孤單的小孩……

Content: and computers were...
Content_zh: 而電腦……

Content: our friends, you know?
Content_zh: 就是我們的朋友，懂嗎？

Content: And so then we were in a safe place where we could be together.
Content_zh: 所以那裡成了一個安全的地方，讓我們可以聚在一起。

Content: So, yeah, we super bonded.
Content_zh: 所以對，我們感情非常好。

Content: Um, all of us.
Content_zh: 我們所有人

Content: I mean, we all still talk to each other and I think we'll talk to each other
Content_zh: 我的意思是，我們仍然互相說話 我想我們會互相說話

Content: for the rest of our lives.
Content_zh: 一直聊到這輩子結束。

Content: Schafer: Yes, we do know each other really well, 'cause she was back at Lucas back in the '90s.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，我們真的很熟，因為她 90 年代也在 Lucas。

Content: So we've known each other for a long time.
Content_zh: 所以我們認識很久了。

Content: You get into a lot of strange, um...
Content_zh: 你在錄音室裡會養成很多奇怪的……

Content: kinda routines in the studio.
Content_zh: 某種例行模式。

Content: When you--it's like-- for some reason, sitting in the recording studio is grueling.
Content_zh: 不知道為什麼，坐在錄音室裡真的很折磨人。

Content: Like, you're just sitting in this little chair, you're hearing voices, but like after a day of that
Content_zh: 你只是坐在一張小椅子上聽聲音，可是一整天下來，

Content: you're like, oh my God, I was in this tiny room all day.

Content_zh: 是啊，這很酷。 這遊戲看起來不錯

Content: I wanna play more.
Content_zh: 我想玩更多。

Content: When you--are you guys gonna have a demo of this game?
Content_zh: 你們會做這款遊戲的 demo 嗎？

Content: Greg: Also meeting today about a PAX demo, um, and what the scope of that will look like.
Content_zh: Greg：今天也要開會討論 PAX demo，以及範圍會是什麼。

Content: Start figuring out a plan for that.
Content_zh: 開始想個辦法吧

Content: Schafer: Okay. Rice: Should be fun.
Content_zh: Schafer：好吧 Rice：應該很有趣。

Content: Kipnis: I would...I would vote against a public demo for now.
Content_zh: Kipnis：我會…… 我現在投票反對公開演示

Content: Schafer: What would we have at our booth, then, if we...just do a trailer?
Content_zh: Schafer：那我們攤位上要放什麼？如果只播預告片？

Content: Rice: Nothing. Schafer: What do you mean, nothing?
Content_zh: Rice：沒什麼 你什麼意思？

Content: Rice: It'd mean we have no games at our booth right now.
Content_zh: Rice：這意味著我們目前沒有遊戲

Content: Schafer: I mean, depends how we show it.
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說，這取決於我們怎麼展示。

Content: Like, showing adventure games on the--on the-- like, at E3 and stuff was always a nightmare,
Content_zh: 在 E3 之類的場合展示冒險遊戲，一直都很麻煩。

Content: 'cause, like, you can't get-- it's not like jumping into a mech and shooting a bunch of stuff, or even playing "Brütal Legend."
Content_zh: 因為它不像跳進機甲到處射擊，甚至也不像玩《Brütal Legend》。

Content: Like, so, you can't-- it's hard to really kind of-- I mean, now that we have-- that headphones are more common
Content_zh: 所以，你不能…… 很難真的……

Content: and stuff, I think it's a little easier to demo them.
Content_zh: 和一些東西，我想這有點容易 演示他們。

Content: But you're still, like-- sitting there for a while being stuck on a puzzle is not something that's fun to do,
Content_zh: 但你還是…… 坐在那一陣子 被困在一個拼圖上 不好玩的事

Content: and people are lining up behind you to see the game.
Content_zh: 人們排在你們後面看比賽

Content: Petty: The big problem with this whole thing, potentially, is that we're gonna disrupt all this--
Content_zh: Petty：這整件事最大的問題 可能是我們要打斷這一切……

Content: we just spent all this effort getting everything into a flow
Content_zh: 我們才剛花了這麼多力氣，把所有事情導入一個流程，

Content: that's going to unblock our dependencies.
Content_zh: 讓相依工作不再卡住。

Content: And so even doing any of this work is probably going to disrupt that somewhat.
Content_zh: 所以即使做這項工作 也可能會打亂這一點

Content: It's hard to say how much until we look at that.
Content_zh: 在我們看完之前 很難說出多少

Content: But, um, polishing the spaceship to beta right now, uh, I think would be the absolute worst thing,
Content_zh: 但是現在把太空船打磨到 Beta，我覺得會是最糟的選擇，

Content: because we have so much areas of the game that have issues we've gotta work through.
Content_zh: 因為遊戲裡還有太多區域有問題要處理。

Content: And people like myself and Bagel, some of the animators, need to stay out ahead
Content_zh: 像我和巴格爾一樣的人 有些動畫家需要留在前面

Content: or we're gonna get exactly where we were, where everything's blocked up, and part of that happened
Content_zh: 否則我們會得到我們的確切位置，那裡的一切被阻擋了，而其中的一部分發生了

Content: because of the last trailer we did.
Content_zh: 就是因為上次做預告片。

Content: I mean, obviously, there's an undeniable benefit.
Content_zh: 顯然有不可否認的好處

Content: It's hard to quantify the benefit, is always the issue with making these decisions, right?
Content_zh: 做這種決定的問題一直都是：好處很難量化，對吧？

Content: 'Cause, like, well, people get excited and that generates goodwill, but...
Content_zh: 因為人們會很興奮 產生善意 但是……

Content: Schafer: Like, the two things that I want spoil each other.
Content_zh: Schafer：我想要的兩件事會互相破壞。

Content: Like, showing really meaty gameplay and not spoiling things are, like,
Content_zh: 想展示很有內容的 gameplay，又不想劇透，

Content: kinda mutually exclusive.
Content_zh: 有點相互排斥

Content: Rice: All right. This is all good stuff.
Content_zh: Rice：好吧 這都是好東西

Content: Schafer: Except you're doing the smile.
Content_zh: Schafer：除非你在笑

Content: You're doing the "I'm really unhappy" smile.
Content_zh: 你露出那種「我真的很不爽」的笑容。

Content: Rice: No, I'm not! I, like-- Schafer: "Yeah, life's shit like I thought.
Content_zh: Rice：不，我不是！ 我，就像，Schafer："是的，生活就像我想的狗屎。

Content: Yep, that's exactly what I thought life was like." Rice: No, I mean, I've always been about trying to, like,
Content_zh: 對，我就是這麼想的" 不 我是說 我一直想……

Content: figure out how we can do it without disrupting.
Content_zh: 想想我們怎麼能不打斷它

Content: But, uh, now we just have to figure out what that means for our PAX plans, so...
Content_zh: 但是 現在我們得弄清楚這對我們的 PAX 計劃意味著什麼 所以……

Content: Schafer: I mean, in the end it's not even about it being disrupting.
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說，到最後其實甚至不是會不會打亂流程的問題。

Content: I really don't care about disrupting Lee at all.
Content_zh: 我真的不在乎打擾李

Content: I mean, it's really-- that does--no.
Content_zh: 我的意思是，它真的 - 那真的 - 不。

Content: No, I mean, it's not even about that.
Content_zh: 不是，我是說甚至不是那個問題。

Content: It's like, if we really felt like, oh man, it was such a juicy reward
Content_zh: 如果我們真的覺得，哇，最後的回報非常大，

Content: at the end of it, like, if it was really, like, that demo would really turn people, like,
Content_zh: 如果那個 demo 真的能讓

Content: millions of people into fans of the game, like, I feel like it would be great.
Content_zh: 幾百萬人成為遊戲粉絲，那我會覺得很棒。

Content: But I feel like showing the spaceship to the press would not necessarily convert
Content_zh: 但我覺得向媒體展示太空船 不一定會改變

Content: a whole bunch of people, you know, um...
Content_zh: 一羣人，你知道，嗯……

Content: Petty: Well, that's what I meant by disruptive: Is it worth it?
Content_zh: Petty：我就是這個意思 值得嗎？

Content: Because like you said in the earlier episodes, no one cares how well a game is produced.
Content_zh: 因為就像你前幾集說的，沒人在乎一款遊戲製作得多順。

Content: I don't think anyone cares how well a game is promoted if it comes out and it's not good.
Content_zh: 如果遊戲推出後不好玩，我也不覺得有人會在乎它宣傳得多好。

Content: Schafer: The one--at least the one discipline here that we can agree no one cares about is production.
Content_zh: Schafer：至少我們可以同意，這裡有一個沒人在乎的職能，就是製作管理。

Content: Crook: You said both those things. Schafer: I don't remember saying that.
Content_zh: Crook：你都說了這些話 我不記得說了

Content: Petty: But, I mean, it's the same thing.
Content_zh: Petty：但是，我是說，這是同一件事。

Content: Like, good production/promotion, if it's not-- if we--you know, I want it to be as good as it can be.
Content_zh: 如果不是…… 如果我們……

Content: Rice: It's just a hard--like, it became a lot more important with all the press shit that went down
Content_zh: Rice：這真的很難。發生那些媒體風波之後，這件事變得重要很多，

Content: 'cause, like, now what does it look like, oh, suddenly we're taken off the floor map of PAX
Content_zh: 因為現在看起來會像是，喔，我們突然從 PAX 平面圖上消失，

Content: and we pulled out of PAX? Like, that looks bad.
Content_zh: 然後退出 PAX？那看起來很糟。

Content: Schafer: That's 'cause I thought someone was gonna throw acid in my face.
Content_zh: Schafer：因為我以為有人會往我臉上扔酸

Content: You can say that: "Tim was worried about acid." Rice: But, yeah.
Content_zh: 你可以這樣說：「Tim 擔心被潑酸。」Rice：不過，對。

Content: Petty: Okay, but then what if we showed a press demo that wasn't totally polished?
Content_zh: Petty：好吧，但如果我們展示的 媒體演示沒有完全打磨？

Content: Which is worse?
Content_zh: 哪個更糟？

Content: Giving 'em ammo to hate on?
Content_zh: 給他們攻擊我們的彈藥？

Content: I think that's worse.
Content_zh: 我覺得這樣更糟

Content: Schafer: Honestly, I--if I could do anything right now, it would just be sit out this PAX.
Content_zh: Schafer：老實說，如果現在我能選，我會直接不參加這次 PAX。

Content: Rice: Yeah, we-- Schafer: I feel like we just don't-- Rice: I think we might have to if we don't do a demo.
Content_zh: Rice：是的，我們…… Rice：如果我們不做演示，我們可能不得不做。

Content: Schafer: I don't know if-- can-can we, now?
Content_zh: Schafer：我不知道…… 能不能……

Content: Rice: We'd probably have to pay for some stuff.
Content_zh: Rice：我們可能得付錢

Content: Petty: We could have a kickass merch booth.
Content_zh: Petty：我們可以弄個超讚的周邊攤位。

Content: Rice: Uh, yeah...we could do that.
Content_zh: Rice：嗯，是的…… 我們可以做到的。

Content: [Music] Bagel: Uh, I think it's going good.
Content_zh: [音樂] Bagel：呃，我覺得進展不錯。

Content: It's pretty rad.
Content_zh: 滿酷的。

Content: I think we've all, like, gotten a lot better at art, you know, over the last year.
Content_zh: 我想我們過去的一年裏 藝術都變得好很多了

Content: So lookin' at all the old stuff is, like, kinda hard, but we all see stuff, I think, that we know we wanna change.
Content_zh: 所以看那些老東西是挺難的 但是我們都看到了 我們知道我們想改變

Content: And, um, it's getting to an exciting time right now.
Content_zh: 而且，嗯，它得到了一個令人興奮的時刻現在。

Content: Uh, it's polish phase, you know, just figuring out what we're gonna polish and just lookin' back
Content_zh: 現在是打磨階段，要弄清楚哪些東西要打磨，回頭看

Content: at all of our old stuff and bringing it all together into an awesome game, you know?
Content_zh: 我們以前做的所有東西，把它們整合成一款很棒的遊戲。

Content: But, yeah.
Content_zh: 但是，是的。

Content: I feel like it's goin' pretty good.
Content_zh: 感覺挺好的

Content: Pretty exciting, but it is also super scary that we only have, like, what, four months left.
Content_zh: 滿令人興奮的，但也超可怕，因為我們只剩大概 4 個月了。

Content: Right? But, yeah, I'm gonna go-- I'm goin' out to San Francisco on the 16th of September,
Content_zh: 對吧？不過我 9 月 16 日要去舊金山，

Content: and, um, I'm gonna be out there for two weeks, and we are gonna, like, talk about polish,
Content_zh: 我會在那邊待兩週，我們會討論打磨，

Content: figure out a plan, and then just start nailing it.
Content_zh: 想想計劃 然後開始搞定它

Content: [Music] Schafer: We've got some professional voice in the game now.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：現在遊戲裡有一些專業配音了。

Content: You can hear--uh, and it's awesome, 'cause they're good.
Content_zh: 你聽得到…… 而且很棒 因為他們很棒

Content: Black: Oh, my!
Content_zh: Black：天啊！

Content: A fresh face rises from below.
Content_zh: 新的面孔從下面升起

Content: Before you ask, let me answer.
Content_zh: 在你問之前，讓我回答

Content: Yes, you may live here in Meriloft forever.
Content_zh: 是的，你可以永遠住在 Meriloft。

Content: Rice: Yeah, it's one of those things that, uh...
Content_zh: Rice：是的，那是其中之一，呃……

Content: changes, uh, the production values of the game in a very short amount of time.
Content_zh: 在很短的時間裏 改變了遊戲的製作價值

Content: Like, 'cause it kind of all comes in at once, in one big chunk,
Content_zh: 因為它幾乎會一次進來，一大批進來，

Content: and suddenly you go from having very non-professional acting to very professional acting.
Content_zh: 你突然從非常不專業的演戲 變成非常專業的演戲

Content: And it--and it just, uh...
Content_zh: 和它 - 它只是，呃……

Content: I think it just reminds you it's real.
Content_zh: 我想它只是提醒你它是真實的。

Content: Schafer: A huge difference.
Content_zh: Schafer：非常不同

Content: It's--it's huge. I mean, we've been looking at these temporary cut scenes for so long where
Content_zh: 很大 我的意思是，我們一直在看這些 暫時剪切的場景

Content: the characters are all moving-- 'cause they don't wanna put a lot of work into it until they have the final VO.
Content_zh: 角色都還只是大概動一動，因為在有最終 VO 前，他們不想投入太多工。

Content: Now that we have the final VO, they can just go to town.
Content_zh: 現在有了最終 VO，他們就可以放手大做。

Content: And so we're gonna see, um, a whole different game experience
Content_zh: 所以我們會看到 完全不同的遊戲經驗

Content: where it just feels so much more polished.
Content_zh: 整體感覺會打磨得多。

Content: Rice: And in a game that's so much about character and story, um, like, it's gonna be really important
Content_zh: Rice：在一場關於人物和故事的遊戲中 這將是非常重要的

Content: that that is sold correctly and those actors are selling it right.
Content_zh: 一定要把那些東西正確傳達出來，而演員也要演到位。

Content: So, you know, Tim's an awesome writer, and now to have, like, people who are able
Content_zh: 所以，你知道，蒂姆是個很棒的作家，而現在，有，像，人是有能力的

Content: to take what he wrote and, like, even make it better is pretty amazing.
Content_zh: 拿走他寫的東西 甚至讓它變得更好

Content: Schafer: And "Act 1" of our game is now in alpha, which is exciting.
Content_zh: Schafer：而我們遊戲的《第一幕》現在進入 Alpha 了，很令人興奮。

Content: [Offscreen] Woo! And--woo!
Content_zh: 哇！ 然後……

Content: But it's lookin' good.
Content_zh: 但是看起來不錯

Content: Rice: Cheers.
Content_zh: Rice：乾杯

Content: [Music] Schafer: It's gonna be fun when we can actually, um,
Content_zh: [音樂] 當我們真的可以……

Content: celebrate, you know, shipping.
Content_zh: 慶祝遊戲推出。

Content: And--but it'll be kind of sad, 'cause it's been kind of fun to do all this and...
Content_zh: 但會有點悲傷 因為做這些事很有趣……

Content: [Music] I'm not gonna celebrate till the whole thing ships.
Content_zh: [音樂] 整個遊戲推出前，我都不會慶祝。

Content: I suppose we'll have some sort of celebration on shipping "Act 1."
Content_zh: 我想《第一幕》推出時，我們還是會稍微慶祝一下。

Content: Then I'm like, don't talk to me until it's done.
Content_zh: 那我就想，在事情結束之前別跟我說話

Content: April/May. April/M-- Definitely between April and November.
Content_zh: 4 月或 5 月。4 月或……反正一定在 4 月到 11 月之間。

Content: Depends on how many the first act sells.
Content_zh: 要看第一幕賣得怎麼樣。

Content: If it sells a lot, we'll just go all the way into 2016.
Content_zh: 如果賣得很好，我們就一路做到 2016 年。

Content: Just kidding.
Content_zh: 開玩笑的

Content: [Music] Kaufman: Okay, that's all we can do.
Content_zh: [音樂] 好了 我們只能這樣了

Content: We're pulling out.
Content_zh: 我們要撤了。

Content: Don't make the same mistake you did last time, boy.
Content_zh: 別犯上次犯的錯，孩子

Content: I-I don't understand.
Content_zh: 我不明白

Content: What is it about this creature?
Content_zh: 這個生物到底有什麼特別？

Content: Brown: Excellent.
Content_zh: Brown：很好

Content_zh: Schafer：我們給粉絲加點料，把台詞剪在一起，做成那種超下流的

Content: "Spock and Kirk having sex" video.
Content_zh: 「Spock 和 Kirk 做愛」影片。

Content: I like to put stuff in there for them.
Content_zh: 我喜歡幫他們塞這種東西。

Content: "Pull out now, boy." Brown: I just...
Content_zh: 「現在拔出來，孩子。」Brown：我只是……
